criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking trade allocations as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP ($7,800,000 in excess profits) and Chao's personal account ($438,000 in excess profits), with statistical evidence (t-statistics of 4.73 and 5.21 respectively), covering the Review Period 2021-2023. This is treated as a primary allegation category throughout the document.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites both Section 206(1) and Section 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the applicable law lists both provisions and the analysis and conclusions repeatedly reference these sections as the statutory basis for the cherry-picking violations.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. In the Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section, the output explicitly states 'WCA executed 4,312 omnibus equity trades through its account at Meridian Clearing Corp.' and references 'the Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA, Whitmore, and Chao engaged in cherry-picking trades.' The Device, Scheme or Artifice to Defraud section also explicitly names WCA alongside Whitmore and Chao as engaging in cherry-picking trade allocations.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Whitmore as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation and specifically mentions his 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP. In the 'Individual Sanctions for Whitmore' section, the output states: 'Whitmore holds a 62% membership interest in WCA and a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP' and notes that 'This personal financial stake created significant incentives for Whitmore to favor Oceanic Fund I LP in allocation decisions.' The 'Conflict of Interest' section also references his '12% personal limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP' as creating a direct conflict of interest with respect to trade allocation decisions.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Chao Identified as Implicated (Personal Account)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Diana L. Chao as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to her personal account receiving favorable allocations. Multiple sections explicitly mention this: the Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section states her personal account had a t-statistic of 5.21 (p-value < 0.001) and received $438,000 in excess profits; the Individual Sanctions for Chao section details that she 'received 87 allocations from omnibus fills in her personal brokerage account' (Account No. ending -7823) and 'personally benefited from the cherry-picking pattern by receiving an outsized share of profitable trade allocations in her personal account, resulting in... her personal account received additional profits of $438,000 over the review period.'
